In the poker scenario, the author is dealt two cards from a standard 52-card deck. To calculate the probability of getting at least one heart in the next 5 cards, we use the complement rule. The total number of ways to choose 5 cards from the deck is C(52, 5). The number of ways to choose 5 cards without any hearts is C(36, 5). The probability of getting at least one heart is 1 minus the probability of getting no hearts, which is 1 - [C(36, 5) / C(52, 5)]. This simplifies to 1 - [1144066 / 2598960] ≈ 0.566.

For the decision-making analysis, the expected value of Strategy A is 0.4 * 1000 + 0.6 * (-500) = 400 - 300 = 100. For Strategy B, it is 0.6 * 800 + 0.4 * (-200) = 480 - 80 = 400. The author should choose Strategy B as it has a higher expected value.
